Field.Label: The plugin finds ESPHome devices on your LAN via mDNS (Bonjour) and creates one Indigo device per ESPHome node. Every entity that node exposes — sensors, switches, buttons and the rest — becomes a state on that single device.
Auto-create Indigo devices on discovery:
Field.Description: Untick to keep discovery running without creating anything. Devices you have already added keep working.

Field.Label: Ignore these devices:
Field.Description: Comma or space separated MAC addresses, hostnames or IP addresses. Listed devices are never connected to and never warned about — use this for hardware that advertises like an ESPHome node but is not one (a SMLIGHT Zigbee coordinator, for example). The 'List Seen Devices' menu item shows each device's MAC.

Field.Label: The default key is read from IndigoSecrets.py first (key name ESPHOME_DEFAULT_ENCRYPTION_KEY, in /Library/Application Support/Perceptive Automation/). The field below is the fallback for anyone who does not use that file.
ESPHome devices may use an API encryption key. The key is per device, so set it in the device's Configure dialog after discovery. The default below is used for any device that has no key of its own.
Default API Encryption Key:
Field.Description: Base64, from the device's YAML 'api: encryption: key:' line. Leave blank to connect without encryption.
